title: What The Trees Said To The Little Boy
content:
I.

    Once when the park
    Was very dark
    I slipped out and went walking;
    And heard the trees
    To the summer breeze,
    And to each other talking.

II.

    And I heard them say,
    "We have stood all day
    In one spot here, and worried
    To keep the sun
    From each little one
    Who laughed at our feet or hurried.

III.

    "Now every boy
    And girl and toy
    Is safe at home, my patience!
    Why! I and you,
    As their parents do,
    Can talk of our relations."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Madison Julius Cawein, titled "What The Trees Said To The Little Boy", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
